qa_pairs.question: When launching a Linux virtual machine, what is the purpose of an SSH key pair and how is it selected within the OpenStack interface?
qa_pairs.answer: When creating a virtual machine in OpenStack, password authentication is disabled for security reasons. Instead, one SSH (Secure Shell) public key is installed on the VM, allowing you to log in using the corresponding SSH key pair. You select the SSH key pair from the 'Available' list under the 'Key Pair' option by clicking the upwards arrow on the far right of its row. If you don't have a key pair, you can create or import one from this window.

qa_pairs.question: In an OpenStack project, what is the distinction between a private IP address and a public IP address for virtual machines, and what is each used for?
qa_pairs.answer: In an OpenStack project, a private IP address (typically in the form 192.168.X.Y) is assigned to a virtual machine (VM) from a local network. This network connects VMs to each other and to an internet gateway within the project, enabling communication between VMs but not allowing connections from the outside world. A public IP address (also referred to as a Floating IP), on the other hand, allows outside services and tools to initiate contact with your VM, which is useful for administrative tasks, serving web content, or pointing domain names to your VM.

qa_pairs.question: After launching a virtual machine in OpenStack, what are the steps to assign a public IP address to it?
qa_pairs.answer: To assign a public IP address (Floating IP) to your virtual machine in OpenStack, first ensure you are viewing the instances list. Locate your VM, click on the drop-down arrow menu (▼) on the far right of its row, and select 'Associate Floating IP'. In the 'Allocate Floating IP' window, click the 'Allocate IP' button. If this is your first time associating a floating IP, you'll need to click the '+' sign in the 'Manage Floating IP Associations' dialog box. Finally, click the 'Associate' button. Your VM will then display both its private and newly assigned public IP addresses in the 'IP Address' column.
